title: ficus parasitica var. tuberculata
intro: Ficus parasitica var. tuberculata, also known as the strangler fig, is a variety of fig plant that grows by forming aerial roots that strangle the host tree, eventually killing it. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ficus parasitica var. tuberculata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make infusions, tinctures, and decoctions that are used to treat various ailments.
